content:
Use the python tool to read this CSV and create a PNG line chart.
Requirements:
- Plot monthly average temperature (Jan-Dec only; ignore Annual Mean)
- Include x/y axes and month labels
- Save the chart as a PNG file

CSV:
Month,Average Temperature (°C),Deviation from 1991-2020 Norm (°C)
January,0.0,+1.5
February,1.3,+1.7
March,4.1,+1.5
April,8.2,+1.9
May,10.3,-0.2
June,17.6,+3.8
July,15.4,-0.3
August,16.4,+1.2
September,11.9,+0.5
October,7.2,-0.2
November,2.3,+0.1
December,1.3,+2.0
Annual Mean,8.0,+1.1

input.code:
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from io import StringIO

csv_data = """Month,Average Temperature (°C),Deviation from 1991-2020 Norm (°C)
January,0.0,+1.5
February,1.3,+1.7
March,4.1,+1.5
April,8.2,+1.9
May,10.3,-0.2
June,17.6,+3.8
July,15.4,-0.3
August,16.4,+1.2
September,11.9,+0.5
October,7.2,-0.2
November,2.3,+0.1
December,1.3,+2.0
Annual Mean,8.0,+1.1
"""

df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(csv_data))
months_order = [
    "January","February","March","April","May","June",
    "July","August","September","October","November","December"
]
df = df[df["Month"].isin(months_order)].copy()
df["Month"] = pd.Categorical(df["Month"], categories=months_order, ordered=True)
df = df.sort_values("Month")

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
plt.plot(df["Month"], df["Average Temperature (°C)"], marker="o", linewidth=2)
plt.xlabel("Month")
plt.ylabel("Average Temperature (°C)")
plt.title("Monthly Average Temperature")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.4)
plt.tight_layout()

output_path = "/mnt/data/monthly_average_temperature.png"
plt.savefig(output_path, dpi=150)
plt.close()

print(output_path)

content.text:
Done — I created the PNG line chart.

[Download the PNG chart](sandbox:/mnt/data/monthly_average_temperature.png)

content: whats the name of the file and how many rows in the csv was there?

content.text:
- File name: `monthly_average_temperature.png`
- Rows in the CSV: `13` data rows, or `14` including the header
